evidence: The site represents a major milestone in the university's enterprise-wide Responsible AI initiative, a multi‑year effort led by the Emory AI Group with input from the Responsible AI Executive Governance Committee and partners across Emory University and Emory Healthcare.

source.name: Responsible AI Initiative
source.notes_excerpt: Multiple organizations operate under the name "Responsible AI Initiative" with different scopes and affiliations. The University of Utah operates the One-U Responsible AI Initiative, which aims to advance AI to achieve societal good while protecting privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties. Emory University launched a Responsible AI website as part of their enterprise-wide Responsible AI initiat

target.name: Emory AI Group
target.notes_excerpt: Research collective within Emory University encompassing multiple AI-focused labs and faculty across the Department of Computer Science and School of Medicine. Includes the Emory NLP group, AI and Simulation Lab (geospatial AI, computer vision, foundation models), MAIX Lab (health AI), and Cardiothoracic Imaging and AI Lab. source.name: Université de Montréal
source.notes_excerpt: Université de Montréal is a French-language institution of higher learning founded in 1878, regarded as one of the most important universities in Canada in terms of research and one of the largest by enrolment. target.name: HEC Montréal

Faculty contribute to Mila and AI governance research.

target.name: IBM
target.notes_excerpt: IBM is a global enterprise technology company offering AI via its watsonx platform. It advocates 'precision regulation'—risk-based, use-case-specific rules rather than broad AI restrictions. IBM reported $12.5B in generative AI bookings as of early 2026 and positions AI governance software as a primary growth driver.

source.name: Element AI
source.notes_excerpt: Element AI was a Canadian multinational artificial intelligence company based in Montreal, Quebec that was founded in October 2016. The company was funded by the Government of Canada for CAD$5 million, and raised US$102 million independently, before being acquired by ServiceNow. target.name: Amnesty International
target.notes_excerpt: Major international human rights organization with strong positions on AI governance and digital rights. Calls for outright ban on facial recognition technology for mass surveillance by police and state agencies, citing racial discrimination (higher false positive rates for women and people of color) and chilling effects on peaceful assembly. Runs the 'Ban the Scan' campaign documenting FRT harms

evidence: With headquarters in Geneva and regional chapters in over 40 countries, IASEAI has become a trusted advisor to the United Nations, the OECD, and the European Union on matters related to AI governance

source.name: International Association for Safe and Ethical AI
source.notes_excerpt: The International Association for Safe and Ethical AI (IASEAI) is a non-profit organization founded in 2024 with headquarters in Paris, France. The organization's stated mission is to address the risks and opportunities associated with advances in artificial intelligence and to ensure that AI systems operate safely and ethically, benefiting all of humanity. target.notes_excerpt: Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (38 member countries). Published OECD AI Principles in 2019 (first intergovernmental AI standard), updated 2024 — now endorsed by 47 jurisdictions. source.name: Ed Markey
source.notes_excerpt: Senator Edward J. Markey (D-Mass.) is a member of the Commerce, Science and Transportation Committee and has served in the U.S. Senate since 2013. He reintroduced the Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act on December 2, 2025, alongside Representative Yvette Clarke, to prevent companies from using biased and discriminatory AI-powered algorithms in critical decisions affecting Americans' lives. target.name: Lawyers' Committee for Civil Rights Under Law

Markey, Rep. Clarke) requiring transparency, accountability, and anti-discrimination safeguards in AI systems used in housing, lending, healthcare, employment, education, and law enforcement.
